---
title: "What's Behind Building a Dojo"
description: "Thoughts about a dojo"
author:
- name: Erwin Lares
date: 2025-06-26
date-modified: last-modified
categories:
  - aikido
  - community
  - personal
status: published
format:
  html:
    include-before-body: "../../_partials/blog-nav.html"
---

I'll confess, I am still pinching myself to make sure I am not dreaming.

It feels unreal to have the opportunity to open a dojo and bring Aikido to the community where I have lived for more than a decade now. A couple of years ago, a friend asked me, "What is your dream?" I reflexively replied - "I would love to be able to have a place that was one half a dojo and the other half a pottery".

I didn't realize the idea of a dojo was already churning in the back of my mind. I have been practicing Aikido for a while now and two things were starting to become evident. I needed more days of training

Aikido of Harvard closing its doors just precipitated this.
